title: Samantha Olinger -Hiring Manager Screen - Andi | Engagement Manager
meeting_title: Samantha Olinger -Hiring Manager Screen - Andi | Engagement Manager

transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hello.
Hi, Andi.
How are you?
I'm all right.
We're a little overwhelmed again, still, whatever.
I feel like that's probably ongoing right now, right?
Yeah.
So sorry, I'm a little late.
Good to see you again.
And thank you for being willing to chat with me again.
Um, everything's moving really fast.
And I feel like the last time we chatted, things probably changed in terms of, like, what this role looks like and how the org is structured.
So I thought it would be good to just kind of reset a little bit and just, like, start from the beginning.
Um, so when, what was the day that we last talked?
Do you remember?
Was it in December or November?
I think it was December.
December.
Okay, but very early on in December, like not much.
Okay, yeah, because early December, basically, like we did a reorg and kind of like redefined what this role was.
And I think we paused everything because we just wanted to see how things would settle out and like what we learned, like what we would learn in terms of like the kind of profile that we needed.

Um, so I think like the things that have come out from that, and I was just opened up our kind of like last conversation a little bit, but the things that have come out from that are really like, um, I think we're thinking more about the commercial mindset of this role.
Um, because, you know, there's now a number associated with, um, like, these renewals and upsells in terms of these accounts, um, kind of like part of the total comp package.
Uh,

And, like, having, it's really important to have, like, that editorial and SEO, like, experience to come across to clients as that expert, but, you know, pairing it with that commercial conversation, we're doing a lot of training around that for the existing team, just because it wasn't kind of something that we, people had to balance.
And so I think, like, we're hopeful that as we're hiring, we get people that have kind of done both.

So, like, with that in mind, I think we talked a lot about, like, organization and, like, prioritization and talking and, like, thinking about kind of structuring, like, the client strategy and engaging the client, but would love to, like, hear maybe your background with, like, that commercial mindset.
A little bit more forward, so if you want to, maybe we start there and you kind of talk to me about kind of your experience.
And then I won't ask you all of the same questions, a lot of the questions are the same, but we can kind of, we can start there.
But do you have any questions for me before we jump in, since we have talked before?
I don't think so right now.
You know, I'm kind of excited to hear, though, that it's, it expands to that, right?
That, you know, because this sounds like more, like you guys are determining it to be more of like a hybrid, where it is kind of like account management more so.
And like you said, that renewal, the upsell, you know, diving into that, but also having some of that SEO, and, you know, even just now some of that, like AI engine expertise, too.
So, yeah, awesome.
Yeah.
Cool.
Well, with that, would love to hear about your.
So I'm trying to think of where to go from here.
So from the commercial aspect, I mean, that is, you know.
So that's what I'm taking from this is you're talking more about like reducing the churn and, you know, like how to expand who's there and everything.
Okay.
So my experience there is, is pretty, it's pretty extensive, right?
We own our customers at BrightEdge.
Typically when I was with the agency, we had a little bit less of that, right?
It was a little bit more of like, oh, hey, I noted this opportunity and I would pass it along.
But here I've had the opportunity to really like dive in.
For example, tomorrow afternoon, I'm like running a demo on an expansion for a financial institution, right?
So they can see, you know, like, hey, is this going to work for us?
So with that demo too, we're giving them like five days of the product to see if it's, you know, fits their needs.
I can almost tell you what 100% does, right?
Like that's why I was like, let's just do the demo.
And I like to take that approach when it comes to things like that.
With the upsells, give them that little taste of it, because they feel like that really hooks them, and then they're sold on it.
With expansion, it is sometimes a little bit more difficult, because you're looking at outside, things that you typically don't get into too much in conversation.
But last year, I had an expansion with one of my current customers, and they have a secondary company that they work with.

They're not technically partners, but they work alongside of them, so we were able to take some of the things that we want with them, and say, hey, this is some of the improvements that we help them make, show them those things, and then sign both of those customers into one package there.
So that was nice.
So yeah, like I said, mean, we own everything here, so I've had the opportunity to really learn how to see those keys and be really proactive.
Like, hey, I know you're going to need this in the next couple.
Let's start, you know, kind of putting it into the conversation now, getting into your head.
So you feel like you asked me that you need these things.
That's, yeah, that's perfect.
I think that's something that, like, we're coaching on a little bit is, like, the timing of, like, like, hard sell versus that, like, idea planting inception piece.
So great to hear that you've done some of that before.
Can you talk to me about, like, red flags that you watch out for that signal that a client might not be healthy?
So, of course, there's always, you know, the big one, POC turnover, right?
That's the easiest one.
And sometimes they come to you with it, right?
Sometimes they're like, hey, I'm leaving.
And you're like, oh, my gosh, you've been my champ for two years.
Like, I don't know who else to talk to.
Sometimes, you know, it's unexpected.
You get on a call and somebody new is there and they're like, oh, yeah, we lost this person.
And I think really I've learned.
And to kind of navigate through that, again, by being proactive and really making sure that even if someone is a solo team, they have someone else that they're accountable to, right?
And making sure that that person is at least included in some conversations.
So that way, too, you have at least a minor relationship with leadership in some way.
So if something like that does happen, you know you're not just, they're not just going to be like, well, we're going to fold because they were the only people that knew how to use this.

Some of the other health signals, too, of course, adoption and, you know, even just general engagement, whether it's on a session or just like, hey, I'm sending you an email because we have something new and you're not even responding to me or you're just putting it off, right?
And I think the best way that I've learned to navigate through a lot of that is something that people really tend to not do.
And I will pick up the phone.
It's so interesting to see how someone will decline a meeting or tell me that they're unavailable.
But if I pick up the phone.
Call them, they will answer.
And I know sometimes to some people that seems a little invasive, but they're like, all right, I get your point, you know, kind of calms that.
But like I said, I think really my biggest strategy has just been to make sure that there's a larger portion of the team as a whole or the company as a whole involved.

And even if it's just sharing wins with somebody like, hey, you know, like kudos to you guys and what your team did so that we have, you know, multiple points of contact if someone is becoming disengaged.
Yeah, I was gonna dive into that.
I'm kind of glad you mentioned that.
Like, we've had a couple of clients that have been acquired or like had that POC turnover.
So what are the things that you do to try and kind of either engage with new stakeholders or do some org mapping?
Like, what are your tricks?

So I think, you know, like I said, especially when there is something of value to show, especially when you can tie it back to a really specific goal and tell a story for them, right, in that quick snapshot, right?
A lot of times it's that, hey, here's a quick screenshot, and like, this is what it is, and calling out that big number, right?

So like recently, I just sent over one of those to a customer, and they're in like the hobby industry, and one of their, one of the projects we've been working on, they saw like a 250% increase in their conversion rate, right?
So basically from nothing to everything.
And I mean, just that's the title, right?
So you know, and of course, making sure that you're like, make sure someone opens this, right?

And I mean, like, typically, those are the kind of things that get a response, because you're like, oh, hey, like, if you put somebody, you know, if you put in the title, like 250% increase in your, you know, conversions, people.
And typically, that's when leadership will be like, oh, that's great to see, even though it's a small engagement.
Outside of that, too, I do always ask my POC or whoever I'm in contact with, hey, have you talked with your team?
Does this matter to your team?
And how high up are we talking?
Are we talking to your general team?
Or do you need somebody else involved in this conversation, especially when they're having trouble identifying goals?
Or determining how to go forward on projects?
Yeah.
Yeah.
The identifying goals thing is so interesting.
So many people are tracking goals and or have a goal in their head, like a secret goal that they're like, no, no, it's not.
And then like, they either won't tell us or, or they're not really tracking it.
We had a client last week that was like, I want to let you know that like, this growth is fine, but it's not as fast as we would have wanted.
Wow.
And I was like, what?
Really?
Okay, well, like, what's the number that you're aiming at?
And she's like, I don't have one.
I just want it faster.
And I was like, cool.
I know.
So I have tried to get better at asking, like, specifics, So I had recently somebody that was like, well, we need more leads.
And I was like, okay, well, first off, what quantifies a lead?
Like, let's talk about what quantifies a lead to you versus what it is to me.
And then, you know, like, when you say you need more, where are we at now?
And like, are we talking a percentage we need to see?
Do we need, do you need to give these people a thousand leads?
Like, what do you need?
Yeah, yeah.
Uh, clients, when they're paying you money, they feel like they can have everything.
Um, okay, well, those were just like the two additional questions.
And, um, yeah, I just wanted to reconnect.
So thank you.
I thank you for your time and for your patience.
Um, what questions?
questions?
Questions do you have for me?
Like updated questions.
It's really funny, because I think you guys maybe answered them, because some of the things I thought about was like, were those kind of things?
Like, how are you, you know, going to be like measuring success for this role?
And like, what are some, you know, success metrics that like your clients are looking for where you're seeing a gap?
Yeah.
Yeah, we actually were like, we're refining our health scoring right now.
We have sort of, we have like five pieces that were like judging health on, but we don't have sort of a really tight rubric on them yet.
So relationship, like quantity, kind of like, are we meeting the like contractual velocity?
performance, and, oh my god, there are two more.
Performance, quantity, relationship, quality, obviously.
And then there's one more that is like related to performance and quantity.
And I'm so sorry that I'm not remembering it right now.
But yeah, so it's like one to five, each of those are scored.
And then kind of like anything under 20 is we talk about in a weekly health meeting.
But yeah, we're still calibrating.
And I should know those cults.
So you've only had a bunch to learn though.
Yeah.
But that's, yeah, that's the way that we're thinking about those today.
And really like using this time of the year to be like, what are your goals?
And also, you know, we've been leveraging GA4 and GSC for clients a lot.

But starting to get like, give us access to your HubSpot, if that's what you're using, give us access to your post hog, like, what else are you using that you again, like, what's your secret number that you're tracking without telling us about.
So we're being like really explicit about uncovering those for the year ahead.
Yeah, yeah.
No, I can't understand.
Yeah.
I think I've learned over the years.
Yeah.
Like, you know, if I ask people like, hey, what conversions do you actually have set up?
Like, it's helped me to be like, oh, that's what you need to know.
Yeah.
So, yeah.
But I think that, like I said, that was really the questions that I had.
And then, I mean, are you guys using any, like, anything that's measuring health score?
Or are you coming up with something on your own?
Or?
So we were looking at some, a couple of different tools, but now we're just using, like, we're building out a system.
And then we, not me, Leah, who works on ops for the team, is doing like an MCP server to Claude, which I have to play around with this week, which is like, hey, what are the flags?
Like, let us know.
So Claude is maybe doing some extra analysis.
But yeah, just like at this point, it's more basic kind of like projections and health, like sort of renewal projections based on health scoring a little bit.
Okay.
So that's like how you're forecasting right now.
Yeah.
Okay.
All right.
Yeah, I think that's really it on my end then.
Okay.
Awesome.
Well, again, thank you for taking the time again.
You're going to speak, I think, with Megan next, who has worn a couple of different like hats on all like account facing.
So she's like seeing some of...
The changes and, you know, been here when there was no guidance and now like helping to do a lot of like the structure and strategy for the team.
So she'll probably be a really good person for you to chat with.
And hopefully we can make this happen soon.
So I'm happy that you're still interested and available.
No, I mean, honestly, like you guys, some of the things that you've been doing and like watching, you know, kind of the company itself.
I'm like, and even at first I was really interested.
So that's why I was kind of like, I kept coming back.
I'm like, hey, yeah, well, I've been interviewing with some other people and like things are progressing there too.
And I was like, just want know.
Well, we're in a pretty big hurry these days.
We've got somebody going out on maternity leave in a couple of months too.
So, um, yeah, definitely could use the help.
So awesome.
Uh, hearing back from you guys, but if you need anything.
Just let me know any clarity on anything or, you know, whatever it happens to be.
All right.
Perfect.
Thanks, Sam.
All right.
Thanks.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Screening for a redefined Engagement Manager role with a new commercial focus.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=486.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Role Redefined:** A December reorg added a commercial focus to the Engagement Manager role, tying compensation to renewals and upsells.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=486.0)
  - [**Candidate Fit:** Sam's experience at BrightEdge aligns with this, including proactive upsells (e.g., a 5-day demo hook) and multi-stakeholder engagement to reduce churn.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=657.0)
  - [**Next Step:** Sam will interview with Megan, who has a deep understanding of the team's evolution and new strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1364.0)
  - [**Urgent Need:** The company is hiring quickly to cover an upcoming maternity leave.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1412.0)

## Topics

### Role Redefinition

  - [A December reorg redefined the Engagement Manager role, adding a commercial focus to the existing SEO and editorial expertise.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=486.0)
  - [**New Responsibilities:**](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=506.0)
      - [Renewals & upsells](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=506.0)
      - [AI engine expertise](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=617.0)
  - [**Compensation:** Total comp package now includes a metric tied to renewal and upsell performance.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=506.0)
  - [**Hiring Goal:** Find candidates who already balance this commercial and technical skill set.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)

### Candidate Experience: Commercial Mindset

  - [At BrightEdge, Sam owns the full customer lifecycle, including upsells and renewals.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=668.0)
  - [**Upsell Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=688.0)
      - [**Hook:** Offer a 5-day product demo to give clients a "taste" and build confidence.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=691.0)
      - [**Proactive Expansion:** Identify opportunities early, like signing a customer's secondary company to a single package.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=728.0)
  - [**Churn Prevention:**](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=808.0)
      - [**Proactive Engagement:** Involve leadership or other stakeholders early to build relationships and mitigate risk from POC turnover.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=808.0)
      - [**Direct Communication:** Use phone calls for unresponsive clients; they often answer, providing a direct channel to resolve issues.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=867.0)

### Candidate Experience: Stakeholder Management

  - [**Engaging New Stakeholders:**](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=934.0)
      - [**Show Value:** Use data-driven wins to capture attention.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=945.0)
      - [**Example:** A recent email subject line highlighting a "250% increase in conversion rate" secured a response from leadership.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
  - [**Defining Goals:**](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
      - [**Clarify Ambiguity:** When a client requests "more leads," define "lead" and quantify the target (e.g., percentage increase, specific number).](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1091.0)
      - [**Uncover "Secret Goals":** Ask about other tools (HubSpot, Posthog) to find the metrics clients track internally but don't share.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1248.0)

### Internal Processes & Urgency

  - [**Health Scoring:**](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1151.0)
      - [A new system is in development, scoring clients 1–5 on five factors (e.g., relationship, performance).](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1164.0)
      - Clients scoring \<20 are discussed in a weekly health meeting.
  - [**Renewal Forecasting:** Uses health scores and projections.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1343.0)
  - [**AI Analysis:** Leah (Ops) is building a system to feed client data to Claude for automated flag detection.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1303.0)
  - [**Hiring Urgency:** The company is hiring quickly to cover an upcoming maternity leave.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1412.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Sam:** Interview with Megan, who has a deep understanding of the team's evolution and new strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/eftEBcuT2fsk4y4SE5Kyj7xQEDsNzuxB?tab=summary&timestamp=1364.0)
